event_blocks.summary: In a modern kitchen, a shirtless man with a defined torso, standing near kitchen counter items like organic sugar and cold-pressed oil, addresses the camera, emphasizing that achieving leanness, such as reducing body fat from 30% to 10%, requires a strong mindset and is a priority. He illustrates this with a split-screen transformation graphic and a tattooed man with a beard showing significant fat loss (27% to 7%), while critiquing common online advice like 'caloric deficit' and 'keto' as 'all lies.' He explains that pain and priority dictate commitment, using the analogy of someone with a broken hand to represent high pain levels and questioning the viewer's own pain regarding their physique goals, even suggesting a need for a wife or girlfriend as a motivator. He highlights that health and fitness must be a top priority, contrasting it with family or business, and that those who realize this achieve the best results. He dismisses quick-fix medical interventions like liposuction or Ozempic for those with only a month to lose weight, and criticizes unsustainable methods like keto diets or water fasts for those committing only two months. He advocates for a longer commitment of three to four months, stressing that body transformation takes time, just as gaining weight did, and requires continuously practicing correct habits linked to mindset and psychology. He concludes by calling the life-changing experience of getting in shape 'pre-rich,' emphasizing that pain and priority, along with time, are crucial factors.

Search Results Screen.description: A dark screen displaying multiple search results for 'how to lose body fat,' with titles and dates, overlaid with a question mark icon, suggesting inquiry or confusion about effective methods.

Tattooed Man with Beard.description: A man with a full beard and visible tattoos on his arms and chest, shown in a before-and-after transformation sequence, wearing patterned and gray shorts, with body fat percentages displayed (27% to 7%), serving as visual evidence supporting the speaker's claims of dramatic physical change.

Glass Display Cabinet.description: A tall, transparent glass cabinet with wooden top and shelves, containing a decorative vase, visible in the background when the man speaks by the window.

Shirtless Man.description: A muscular man with a defined torso, initially seen shirtless in a montage, then in a modern kitchen wearing red athletic shorts and a watch. He speaks directly to the camera with an expressive, motivational, and instructional tone, using hand gestures and touching his chest/abdomen to emphasize points about body transformation. He stresses the importance of patience, time, mindset, and dedication, advising viewers to 'give your body grace,' 'practice the correct habits,' and 'figure out your priority.' He describes getting in shape as a 'life-changing experience' and 'pre-rich.'

Split-Screen Transformation Graphic.description: A side-by-side visual comparison showing the same man before and after weight loss, with the 'before' image showing a softer physique and the 'after' image showing a lean, muscular build, overlaid with the text '-53LBS' on a textured gray background.

Steps Graphic.description: A minimalist white background with the word 'STEPS' in bold black letters, surrounded by numbers 1 through 5 in a circular arrangement, indicating a step-by-step process.

Kitchen Counter Items.description: A collection of kitchen products including a container labeled 'Organic Sugar' and a dark bottle labeled 'Cold Pressed Oil', placed on a marble countertop in the kitchen setting.

Arm Sling Person.description: A person wearing a blue arm sling with light blue trim, shown in a close-up, visually symbolizing injury or pain. This visual directly reinforces the 'PAIN' theme discussed by the Shirtless Man, who uses the analogy of a broken hand to explain the urgency and motivation associated with addressing 'pain' in the context of body transformation.

Text Overlay.description: A white screen with handwritten black text reading 'WHAT IS YOUR TOP PRIORITY?'. This question directly aligns with the Shirtless Man's audio prompt: 'So figure out where this falls in your priority.'

Locker Room Scene.description: A setting with blue lockers and tiled flooring, where the Shirtless Man stands in gray shorts with a blue waistband, posing with hands on hips, suggesting a post-workout or gym environment. This visual supports the overall theme of fitness and physical transformation discussed by the Shirtless Man.

Doctor in White Coat.description: A man in a white lab coat with a stethoscope around his neck, labeled 'DOCTOR', holding a blue pill. This visual appears as the Shirtless Man discusses medical interventions like 'liposuction, gastric bypass, balloon, maybe Ozempic' for those seeking quick results.

Laptop Screen with Clock Graphic.description: A laptop screen displaying a yellow clock with text about '30 minutes of cardio daily' and '1000 calories deficit'. This visual reinforces the Shirtless Man's discussion about different timeframes (one month, two months) and specific strategies for fitness and weight loss.

raw_segments: The video opens in a bright, modern kitchen where a shirtless man, with a defined torso, gestures while speaking directly to the camera. He confidently states, "Watch this. If you finally want to get lean. You want to be able to go from 30% body fat to 10% body fat without a struggle." Kitchen items like organic sugar and cold-pressed oil are visible on the counter. The scene transitions to a split-screen comparison, visually demonstrating his own significant body fat reduction, as he explains he will guide viewers through the entire process with scientific evidence, step by step. He emphasizes that these are the same steps he's used for himself over the last 10 years, improving them with research data and his knowledge as a medical doctor, and also with all his clients. He then poses confidently in various indoor settings, showcasing his toned physique. A 'STEPS' graphic appears, reinforcing his structured approach. Further transformation shots include a tattooed man with a beard, labeled with dramatic body fat percentages (27% to 7%), visually supporting the speaker's claims. A close-up of an eye with a question mark transitions to a screen filled with search results about losing body fat, aligning with his critique of common online advice like 'caloric deficit' and 'keto,' which he dismisses as 'all lies to an extent.' The speaker, now in a different room with a glass display cabinet, encourages viewers to watch till the end, implying commitment is key. He concludes back in the kitchen, arms outstretched, as the text 'STEP 1:' appears, signaling the start of his guide, stating, "The number one thing I see with everybody..."

The segment opens with a close-up of a person's feet on a digital scale, displaying '188', setting a focus on weight. The scene transitions to the Shirtless Man, a muscular figure in red athletic shorts, speaking directly to the camera in a bright, modern kitchen with marble countertops and visible Kitchen Counter Items like 'Organic Sugar' and 'Cold Pressed Oil'. He emphasizes that the key to getting lean 'is number one is here in the mind. And it's the mindset,' as on-screen text reinforces 'STEP 1: MINDSET'. He uses expressive hand gestures, explaining that people who've asked him for advice on how to get into shape often overlook this. He continues, occasionally shifting positions to stand by a large window with a city view and near a Glass Display Cabinet, maintaining a motivational tone. He states there are 'two things that play a massive role' in getting lean, which are 'pain and priority,' aligning with the on-screen text '1. PAIN 2. PRIORITY'. To illustrate 'pain,' the video cuts to a person wearing a blue arm sling, as the Shirtless Man uses the analogy of 'someone with a broken hand' who experiences 'the highest level of pain' and 'want to fix this pain immediately.' He then challenges the viewer, asking, 'how much pain are you in?' regarding their physique goals, even posing the question, 'Do you need to find a a wife or a girlfriend right now and you need to first lose this weight?'

The video opens with a muscular, shirtless man, wearing red athletic shorts and a watch, standing in a modern kitchen. He speaks directly to the camera with a direct, motivational tone, using expressive hand gestures, occasionally touching his chest and abdomen, as he discusses the urgency of addressing health issues. He asks, "Do you need to save your marriage? Is it extremely important to lose this weight because there is a health risk and maybe time is running against you?" He explains that a high amount of pain dictates priority, contrasting it with minor issues like an "elbow hurts" from tennis. Behind him, a marble countertop holds items like 'Organic Sugar' and 'Cold Pressed Oil', with a cityscape visible through a large window and a Glass Display Cabinet in the background. He continues, emphasizing that "how much pain you have will determine how much priority it is." A YouTube-style 'Subscribed' button with a bell icon appears as he urges viewers to ask themselves where their health falls on their priority list, stating, "it's the number one goal I have in my life, it's my health and fitness." He contrasts this with focusing on family or business, before transitioning to a Split-Screen Transformation Graphic. This graphic visually reinforces his message, displaying a 'before' image of a softer physique next to an 'after' image of a lean, muscular build, consistent with his current appearance, with the text '-53LBS' highlighting a significant weight loss. He concludes by stating that for those who realize health and fitness is their number one priority, "Those people are the ones who get the best results."

The video opens with a split-screen transformation graphic, showcasing a 'Tattooed Man with Beard' before and after a significant weight loss, transitioning from a softer physique to a lean, muscular build, highlighted by the text '-53LBS' on a textured gray background. The scene then shifts to a modern, well-lit kitchen where the now 'Shirtless Man,' wearing red athletic shorts and a watch, speaks directly to the camera with a clear, motivational, and slightly intense voice. He emphasizes his points with expressive hand gestures, touching his chest and abdomen as he discusses how his clients listen and cut out excuses like "I don't have time, this is too much, it's too difficult." He asserts that "people who really want something are ready to do whatever it takes in the right manner." Behind him, 'Kitchen Counter Items' like 'Organic Sugar' and 'Cold Pressed Oil' are visible on a marble countertop, with a cityscape through large windows and a 'Glass Display Cabinet' to the side. The video cuts to a white screen displaying the handwritten text 'WHAT IS YOUR TOP PRIORITY?', directly echoing the man's challenge to "ask yourself, write in a list, what's a priority in your life in general? Do this exercise." He returns to the screen, maintaining eye contact and dynamic movements, continuing to urge viewers to identify what's most important, whether it's family, making money, or health. He provides examples of priorities like "spending time with your family is number one," "getting good grades in university is number two," "finding a girlfriend is number three," and "getting in shape is number four," concluding emphatically with "Don't waste your time."

The video opens with the Shirtless Man, a muscular individual in red athletic shorts and a watch, speaking directly to the camera from a modern, well-lit kitchen. He uses expressive hand gestures, touching his chest and abdomen, as he discusses how many people fail in their weight loss journeys, eating terribly and getting upset when the scale doesn't move, leading to more weight gain. He prompts the viewer to "figure out where this falls in your priority," a theme reinforced by a 'WHAT IS YOUR TOP PRIORITY?' text overlay. He then addresses a hypothetical "Mike," stating, "it is in my top two," emphasizing the importance of mindset. The scene briefly cuts to a 'DOCTOR' in a white coat holding a blue pill, visually aligning with the Shirtless Man's audio discussion of quick-fix medical interventions like "liposuction, gastric bypass, balloon, maybe Ozempic" for those who give themselves only "one month." The video transitions to a gym setting where the Shirtless Man lifts weights, followed by a laptop screen displaying a yellow clock graphic with text about '30 minutes of cardio daily' and '1000 calories deficit,' illustrating the specific plans he critiques. He continues to explain that those who commit to "two months" often resort to unsustainable methods like "keto diet, juice cleansing, water fast, Herbalife challenges," leading to failure and pain. A locker room scene with blue lockers shows the Shirtless Man posing, further emphasizing the fitness context. Returning to the kitchen, he concludes by acknowledging that trying and failing is part of life, but then begins to discuss the more successful approach of committing to "three months, four months..." before the audio cuts off. Throughout, the kitchen's marble countertop with 'Organic Sugar' and 'Cold Pressed Oil' containers, and a Glass Display Cabinet, provide a domestic backdrop to his health and fitness discourse.

The video opens with a quick montage of a muscular, shirtless man in various settings, including a locker room and gym, showcasing his defined physique. The scene then transitions to a modern, well-lit kitchen where the man, now wearing red athletic shorts and a watch, stands in front of a marble countertop. He speaks directly to the camera with an engaging, motivational tone, using expressive hand gestures and frequently touching his chest and abdomen to emphasize his points. He explains that body transformation requires patience, stating, "it will take time to be able to get into the goal the shape of my life because it took time for me to gain the weight it didn't happen overnight." He advises viewers to "continuously practice the correct habits necessary so that they can finally get lean," linking this to "mindset and psychology." Behind him, a container labeled 'Organic Sugar' and a dark bottle labeled 'Cold Pressed Oil' are visible on the counter, with a 'Glass Display Cabinet' and a cityscape view through a large window in the background. He continues, urging viewers to "figure out your priority" and "how much time you're willing to dedicate," noting that if fitness is a top priority, "you're in luck." He emphasizes giving oneself "more time" to avoid frustration and giving up, stating that "it is pain and priority, and the second thing is time." He concludes by calling the "life-changing experience" of getting in shape "pre-rich," maintaining a confident and instructional demeanor throughout.

event_blocks.summary: In a modern, well-lit kitchen, a muscular, shirtless man in red athletic shorts explains how to calculate caloric needs for fat loss. He begins by emphasizing the importance of a caloric deficit, stating that 3,500 calories equal one pound of fat, and a daily deficit of 500 calories leads to losing one pound per week. He demonstrates this by subtracting 500 calories from a maintenance intake of 2,800 calories, resulting in a target of 2,300 calories for fat loss. The man then details macronutrient calculations, explaining that 1 gram of protein or carbohydrates equals 4 calories, and 1 gram of fat equals 9 calories. He allocates 200 grams of protein (800 calories) and 50 grams of fat (450 calories) to his daily intake, leaving 1050 calories for carbohydrates, which he calculates as approximately 263 grams. Throughout the explanation, he interacts with a silver laptop displaying a Google Docs document titled 'Calculating a caloric Deficit' and a Google calculator, visually reinforcing the steps. He also references a ChatGPT interface where a TDEE of approximately 2787 calories was calculated for a 'Moderately Active' individual. The man critiques common dieting misconceptions, such as extreme caloric deficits and fear of carbohydrates, highlighting their importance for brain function and training performance. He uses food items like a yellow squash and red eggplant as visual aids. The segment concludes with a transition to a gym setting where he performs a cable row exercise, reinforcing the 'calories out' aspect of a caloric deficit, and a graphic illustrating 'CALORIES IN' vs. 'CALORIES OUT' with a YouTube interface overlay encouraging subscriptions.

raw_segments: The video opens with a muscular, shirtless man, wearing red athletic shorts, speaking directly to the camera in a modern kitchen. He uses expressive hand gestures, touching his chest and abdomen, as he emphatically discusses being "rich in who you can be as a human being" and getting "everyone in your family in shape," urging viewers to "raise the standard for yourself, financially and for your family." Visible on the marble countertop are items like 'Organic Sugar' and 'Cold Pressed Oil'. A YouTube-style 'Subscribe' bar appears briefly. The man then states, "Now that that's out of the way, come with me," as the scene transitions to a white screen with bold black text announcing 'STEP 2: CREATING A CALORIC DEFICIT'. This is immediately followed by a split-screen transformation graphic featuring a client named 'GARY', showing his 'before' (softer physique) and 'after' (lean, muscular build). The video cuts back to the Shirtless Man, now seated at the kitchen counter with a laptop, continuing his instructional explanation. He reveals he'll use Gary's exact metrics to demonstrate how to "really establish a caloric deficit," promising to show the logic so viewers can "copy what I'm doing here" to "get rid of your belly fat and finally get lean." The laptop screen displays a video of himself with '4 CALORIES' text boxes, which he gestures towards while speaking. In the background, a large window offers a city view, and a glass display cabinet holds a decorative vase. He concludes by reiterating the importance of following his method to "finally be able to get lean," emphasizing to "literally copy" his approach. The lighting remains bright and consistent throughout.

The video opens with a muscular, shirtless man in red athletic shorts, standing in a modern, well-lit kitchen. He speaks directly to the camera in a clear, instructional voice with a slight accent, gesturing expressively and pointing to his chest and abdomen as he explains a nutritional process. He instructs viewers to open their laptops, mentioning that links to resources like his website, 'Sculpt by Science.com,' and other calculators will be provided. He details the profile of a client named 'Gary' (male, 45 years old, 196.7 lbs or 89.2 kg, 5'11" or 180 cm, training five times a week) whom he will use as an example. The camera cuts to close-ups of his laptop screen, first displaying a Google Docs document titled 'Creating a caloric Deficit' with 'Gary's Details' and a 'Cheat Sheet' listing calorie values for protein (4), carbs (4), and fat (9). The man continues to speak, occasionally looking at the screen. The video then transitions to a full-screen view of the 'Sculpt by Science' BMR Calculator webpage, where he audibly inputs Gary's details (45 for age, 180 cm for height, 89.2 kg for weight) while explaining the calculation process. He reappears, leaning forward with an engaged expression, continuing his explanation. The kitchen, featuring dark cabinetry, a marble countertop with 'Organic Sugar' and 'Cold Pressed Oil' containers, and a glass display cabinet in the background, maintains a consistent instructional and health-focused tone throughout.

The video opens in a modern, sleek kitchen with dark cabinetry and a marble countertop, where a muscular, shirtless man in red athletic shorts and a watch speaks expressively to the camera. He uses hand gestures, often placing them on his chest and abdomen, to explain how to calculate caloric needs. He interacts with a silver laptop displaying a 'Sculpt by Science' BMR Calculator webpage, which he identifies as being "on my website." He explains the process of calculating how many calories "his body burns at rest," emphasizing the need to get this number, which changes based on height, weight, and gender. He inputs data, selecting 'Sedentary: exercise 1-3 times/week,' and the calculator returns '2785 Calories/Day.' He then explains factoring in activity, using a hypothetical "Gary" who trains five times a week, making him "active." The scene cuts to a Google Docs document titled 'Calculating a caloric Deficit' on the laptop, showing the BMR of 2785, along with maintenance and deficit multipliers, and a section detailing calories per gram for protein, carbs, and fat. He states he will copy the 2785 value into a note, confirming "that is his maintenance." Throughout, the man continues to speak and gesture, with kitchen items like 'Organic Sugar' and 'Cold Pressed Oil' visible on the counter, all under warm, focused lighting that creates a professional and informative atmosphere.

The Shirtless Man, in red athletic shorts, stands in a modern, well-lit kitchen, actively engaging with a laptop on a marble countertop. He speaks clearly, instructing viewers to "go to chat, let's go to the AI," as he gestures expressively, emphasizing points with hand movements on his chest and abdomen. The laptop screen initially displays a Google Docs document titled 'Calculating a caloric Deficit,' containing his personal data and BMR calculation. He explains he's going to "copy the details" from this document and paste them into ChatGPT. The scene transitions to a close-up of the ChatGPT interface as he dictates the prompt: "You are an expert health and fitness coach. Give me the total daily energy expenditure for the following information." He then pastes the details, instructing viewers to "write the exact same thing but with your own details," before pressing enter. The AI responds with a recalculated TDEE of approximately 2787 calories per day, based on his 'Moderately Active' status. The man reads the output aloud, stating, "the AI has given Gary 2,787," reacting with excitement, raising his arms and smiling, clearly pleased with the result. He notes that the numbers are "roughly the same" and emphasizes that "all these numbers are in theory," using the "same equation to calculate this." The kitchen counter features a bag of Mission Reduced Carb Wraps, eggs, and 'Organic Sugar,' suggesting a focus on nutrition, while a glass display cabinet and cityscape view through large windows add to the modern ambiance. The man continues to interact with the laptop, reviewing the calculated caloric deficit and macro breakdown, reinforcing the educational and personal nature of the content.

The segment opens with a muscular, shirtless man, wearing red athletic shorts and a watch, standing in a modern, well-lit kitchen. He speaks directly to the camera with a clear, instructional voice, gesturing expressively as he explains nutritional concepts. He points to a silver laptop on the marble counter, displaying a Google Docs document titled 'Calculating a caloric Deficit,' which includes his personal data and BMR calculation. He discusses "maintenance calories," stating, "Now we know how many calories Gary needs to eat to maintain his weight." He references various calculation results, noting, "My calculator said 2,785, the calculator.net said 2,790, the AI said 2,782." He simplifies these figures, deciding to "round it off to 2,800" for easier understanding. The camera cuts to a close-up of the laptop, showing the Google Docs document and then a ChatGPT interface where he inputs data to calculate TDEE, receiving a response of approximately 2,787 calories for a 'Moderately Active' individual. He emphasizes the importance of knowing "the amount of food and calories you can eat every day to maintain the weight that he currently has," but quickly adds, "we don't want that, we want to lose fat." As he continues to explain, he picks up a large yellow squash and a red eggplant, holding them up as tangible examples of food items, while a bag of Mission Reduced Carb Wraps, 'Organic Sugar,' and 'Cold Pressed Oil' are visible on the counter in the bright, reflective kitchen background.

The video opens with a muscular, shirtless man, wearing red athletic shorts and a watch, standing in a modern, well-lit kitchen. He holds a large yellow squash and speaks directly to the camera in a clear, confident, and educational male voice, explaining that "to lose a pound of fat... it's 3,500 calories." He gestures expressively as he places the squash on the marble countertop, continuing to detail that "to lose a pound in a week, we need to be in a caloric deficit of 3,500 calories a week," which simplifies to "500" calories per day. The scene transitions to a black screen displaying bold yellow text: '1 POUND OF FAT EQUIVALENT 3500kcal', followed by a visual breakdown of seven 500kcal blocks under '7 DAYS', illustrating the weekly caloric deficit strategy. The video cuts back to the Shirtless Man, now interacting with a laptop displaying a Google Docs document titled 'Calculating a caloric Deficit'. He continues his explanation, using hand gestures and occasionally pointing to his chest and abdomen, stating, "we will do 2,800 minus 500, and that should put us in a caloric deficit to lose 1 pound of fat a week." The document shows his personal data, a BMR calculation of 2,800 kcal, maintenance calories of 3,000 kcal, and a 500 kcal deficit target resulting in a 2,500 kcal daily intake. The camera zooms in on the laptop screen, revealing detailed macro breakdowns (4 calories per gram of protein and carbs, 9 for fat) and a circular video overlay of the man speaking, as he reiterates, "I'm going to be in a caloric deficit of 500... So 2800 minus 500 is going to give us 2300." He then picks up a red eggplant, holding it alongside the yellow squash, likely to illustrate food choices. The background features a sleek kitchen with reflective surfaces, a glass display cabinet, and city buildings through large windows, maintaining a clean, professional atmosphere as he educates viewers on creating a caloric deficit for fat loss.

The video opens with a muscular, shirtless man in red athletic shorts speaking directly to the camera in a bright, modern kitchen, gesturing expressively as he explains that "it's not going to make a big difference" but what matters is to "follow the plan." He details how he's "going to give Gary 200 grams of protein," rounding that off, and emphasizes the need to know "how many calories there are." In a modern kitchen with large windows and a marble countertop, a muscular, shirtless man, identified as Mike, wearing red athletic shorts and a watch, speaks directly to the camera with a clear, instructional voice. He gestures expressively, often touching his chest and abdomen, as he discusses nutrition and body composition, specifically advising viewers to allocate "20% of your total calories towards carbs." On the counter, his laptop displays a Google Docs document titled 'Calculating a caloric Deficit,' showing his personal data including BMR (2787), Maintenance (3349), Deficit (2887), and Macros (P:200 / 300 / C: F). He transitions to demonstrating calculations on the Google calculator, explaining, "We determined 2,300 is how many calories I'm going to eat, and I want to eat 20% of that, right? Which is .2." He types '20% of 2300' into the calculator, which yields '460,' stating, "That's going to give you 460 calories, right? That's how much is going to go towards fats." He then calculates grams of fat, explaining, "1 gram of fat is equal to 9 calories, so to figure this out, we're going to do 460 divided by 9." The calculator displays '51.1111111111,' which he simplifies to "50 grams" for ease. Throughout his explanation, on-screen graphics appear, including '20% TOWARDS YOUR CARBS' with illustrations of bread, pasta, and rice, and a 'MACRONUTRIENTS' graphic with icons for protein, carbs, and fats. event_blocks.summary: In a modern kitchen, a muscular, shirtless man in red athletic shorts meticulously tracks his nutrition and weight. He then uses his smartphone to log his meals into a nutrition tracking app, detailing his preference for 97% lean ground beef and explaining how to search for specific food items and their nutritional values. The man also logs yogurt and hot sauce, advising viewers to track all food sources. He weighs a red apple on a digital kitchen scale, logs its nutritional information, and reviews his daily macro intake, expressing satisfaction with his progress. He then discusses his weight tracking using a smartphone app called 'Happy Scale,' inputting his weight and noting trends. He explains that weight fluctuations are normal due to factors like water intake and advises viewers not to get emotionally attached to the number, emphasizing that it's just data. He also recommends taking tape measurements every two weeks for various body parts to track physical progress alongside weight trends.

He emphasizes the importance of consistency in tracking, stating, "as long as you're consistent with the option you choose, no problem." He adjusts the serving size to 300g, explaining that this gives him "exactly 63.5 grams of protein, 10.6 grams of fat," totaling "344 calories," and notes the absence of carbohydrates in beef. While speaking, he gestures toward his chest and abdomen to emphasize his points. He then reviews his daily macro intake on the app, which includes breakfast and lunch entries. On the counter, a black container of ground beef, a carton of Oatly oat milk, and a Mission Reduced Carb Wrap are visible, reinforcing his focus on dietary tracking and low-carb, high-protein choices, all under warm, focused lighting that creates an instructional atmosphere.

The Shirtless Man, wearing red athletic shorts and a watch, stands in a modern kitchen with dark cabinetry and a marble countertop, meticulously tracking his nutrition using a smartphone. He reviews his daily intake on a nutrition app, stating that with everything he's added, it comes out to "686 calories," with "32 carb, 20 fat, 86 grams of protein," expressing satisfaction with these numbers. He explains his philosophy of not tracking "veggies" or "non-starchy veggies" due to their low calorie count. He then picks up a container of 'Creamy Light' yogurt, announcing his intention to add "three of these" by scanning its barcode. He calculates aloud that "one of these is 16.6 grams," and multiplying by three, he determines he will input "49.8" grams into the app. Next, he opens a cabinet, retrieves a bottle of 'Perricone (hot)' sauce, and adds it to his lunch entry, advising viewers to "track your sources and your oils" as it's a common mistake. Throughout these actions, he gestures with his hands while speaking, emphasizing his points, and occasionally glances at the phone screen, which displays detailed nutrient breakdowns and percentages of daily goals. The well-lit kitchen features various food items like eggs, a jar of pickles, and a small bowl of dressing visible on the counter, maintaining a focused, instructional tone as he logs his food intake.

In a modern kitchen with dark cabinetry and marble countertops, a muscular, shirtless man in red athletic shorts and a watch speaks directly to the camera, gesturing expressively on his chest and abdomen. He explains his food tracking process, stating he'll add two servings of a product to reach 30 grams, detailing how he'll weigh it on the scale, spread it on his meal, and then check the app for the remaining amount. He places a red and white 'Perricone (hot)' sauce bottle on a digital kitchen scale, which displays 144 grams. He logs this into his smartphone's nutrition tracking app, which shows sections for 'Lunch', 'Dinner', and 'Snacks', along with his remaining daily macros: 79g carbs, 6g fat, 25g protein, and 435 calories. After confirming he's tracked his 'wrap', he emphasizes the importance of including vegetables and fruit in his diet, announcing, "my fruit for today, I'm gonna have an apple." He then weighs a shiny red apple on the same scale, which reads 180 grams, though he states its weight as "220 grams." He logs this 'Red apple' under 'Snacks' in the app, which updates to show its nutritional information: 96 calories, 24g carbs, 0g fat, and 0g protein. He continues to interact with the app, typing into the 'Search Food' bar and reviewing entries, occasionally glancing at the screen, while kitchen items like 'Organic Wave Rolled Oat', 'ISOPEPT', 'Oatly' oat milk, an eggplant, squash, and eggs are visible on the counter.

A muscular, shirtless man, wearing red athletic shorts and a watch, stands in a modern kitchen with dark cabinetry and a marble countertop. With a clear, instructional voice, he explains his nutrition tracking process using his smartphone. He first weighs an item on a digital kitchen scale, noting its weight as 219 grams, which he then inputs into his Nutrition Tracking App. He then discusses logging a red apple, which visually weighed 180 grams, detailing its nutritional information from the app as 129 calories, 30.8 carbs, 0.4 fats, and 0.6 protein. Gesturing expressively to his chest and abdomen, he explains his approach, likening it to a puzzle. The app interface displays his daily progress, showing he has logged breakfast, lunch, and snacks, and currently has 49 grams of carbs, 6 grams of fat, and 28 grams of protein remaining from his daily allowance. He ponders what he can have for dinner, stating his need for a meal with carbs and a lot of protein but very little fat. He considers adding bread as his main carb source for dinner, planning to scan a barcode for a sandwich. Throughout, he interacts with the app, scrolling through entries and retrieving a Perricone (hot) Sauce bottle from a cabinet, while a Creamy Light Yogurt container is also logged. Various items like an Oatly Oat Milk Carton, an ISOPEPT Protein Container, a Mission Reduced Carb Wraps bag, and a Jar of Pickles are visible on the warmly lit counter, highlighting his focus on a controlled diet within the modern kitchen setting, which also features a refrigerator and a glass display cabinet.

A shirtless, muscular man, wearing red athletic shorts and a watch, stands in a modern kitchen with dark cabinetry and a marble countertop. He interacts with a smartphone displaying a nutrition tracking app, initially selecting 'Original Classic' and adjusting the serving size from 3 slices to 1, which updates the nutritional values to 202 calories, 40.3g protein, 3.8g fat, and 0.4g carbs. He explains that "two slices is 32" and compares it to "45" he had, then experiments with "three slices" which takes him to "48.3," noting it's "a little higher." He then navigates to the app's main dashboard, which shows his daily nutrient goals and remaining macros: 79g carbs, 6g fat, 25g protein, and 435 calories. Gesturing expressively near his chest and abdomen, he poses a question to the camera: "what has a lot of protein, no fats, and no carbs?" He brainstorms options like "tuna" for a "toast with tuna," "egg whites," or just "toast" with "butter spray" and a "shake" (mentioning "one scoop is usually 20 grams with water"). Visible kitchen items like Oatly oat milk, Organic Wave Rolled Oat, and ISOPEPT protein reinforce the nutritional focus.

A muscular, shirtless man, wearing red athletic shorts and a watch, stands in a modern kitchen with a marble countertop, actively engaging with his smartphone. He speaks in a clear, instructional voice, explaining his process of logging food into a nutrition tracking app. He begins by searching for 'Egg whites,' stating, "I'm going to search that," and then calculates protein intake, noting that "one large egg gives me 3.6 grams of protein." He interactively determines he needs "six egg whites" to hit his protein target, exclaiming, "And voilà, guys, there it is. I planned my day." The app interface displays his dinner macro tracker, showing 36 calories, 0g fat, 31g protein, 0g carbs, and 64mg sodium for the egg whites. He reviews his daily nutrient breakdown, gesturing expressively and touching his chest and abdomen as he explains, "I went over by my carbs by one... my protein by 2 grams, and I'm over by 58 calories," but concludes, "I'm happy with that. I'm going to take that to the bank." The kitchen counter displays various items including 'Organic Wave Rolled Oat,' 'ISOPEPT,' 'Oatly' oat milk, a red eggplant, a yellow squash, and a few eggs, which he refers to. He emphasizes the ease of the process, stating, "that's how easy it is," and instructs the viewer, "Go into your fridge and just track this." The background reveals a sleek kitchen with stainless steel appliances, a glass display cabinet, and a living area with large windows, all bathed in bright, natural light.

The Shirtless Man, identified as "Mike" (or addressed as such), wearing red athletic shorts and a watch, stands in a modern kitchen with a marble countertop and dark cabinetry. He speaks directly to the camera in a clear, instructional tone, gesturing expressively and emphasizing points by touching his chest and abdomen, discussing how tracking helps learn about foods like "Chobani yogurt" and figure out "which recipes work for you." He mentions taking something out but not using it. The scene transitions to a title card reading 'STEP 4: DATA TRACKING'. Returning to the kitchen, Mike walks toward a silver digital kitchen scale, which he refers to as "a weight scale" and "this bad boy," placed on the floor. The sound of him stepping onto it is heard as he measures his weight, stating the reading as "186.6." He then uses his smartphone to open a nutrition tracking app, which he identifies as "Happy Scale," navigating to the 'Log My Weight' section. He visually inputs his weight as 165.0 lbs for March 22, 2024, logging the entry, despite having stated his weight as 186.6 lbs. The app interface is displayed, showing the 'Summary' page with 'No Entries Yet' and an 'Add an Entry Here' button. Mike continues to speak, gesturing toward the app and the scale, reinforcing the importance of data tracking in his fitness journey, advising viewers not to get "emotionally attached to the number" because "your weight is literally your mass times gravity. It's just data." The kitchen environment remains consistent, with natural light coming through large windows and a white sofa visible in the background.

The Shirtless Man, a muscular figure in red athletic shorts and a watch, stands in a modern, well-lit kitchen with dark cabinetry and a marble countertop, speaking directly to the camera with a clear, articulate voice. He gestures expressively, emphasizing points on his chest and abdomen, as he explains that weight scales are just for seeing "where you are" and don't "mean anything" because many factors can fluctuate. Holding a green bottle labeled 'LECHINO', he uses it as a prop, hypothetically asking the audience to imagine drinking "this whole bottle" before bed, which could cause their weight to jump from "186.6" to "188.8" by morning, clarifying, "No, you're not fat, it's just you drank more water." He then transitions to demonstrating a nutrition tracking app on his smartphone, stating, "I'm just going to show you guys hypotheticals." He navigates to the 'Log My Weight' screen, explaining he'll "add entry here" and "go to pounds," then selects 'Apr 1, 2023' and visually inputs '165.0 lbs' into the weight field. He continues to discuss hypothetical weight changes, mentioning going from "186.6" to "186 flat" and then "185.6" on subsequent days, maintaining engagement with the audience by occasionally glancing at the phone and then back to the camera. On the counter, a yellow squash, red eggplant, red apple, and a small bowl are visible, but not directly referenced in his speech.

The Shirtless Man, wearing red athletic shorts, stands in a modern kitchen with dark cabinetry and a glass display cabinet, speaking directly to the camera with an enthusiastic and motivational tone. He gestures expressively, emphasizing his points about health tracking. Beside him on a marble countertop are a smartphone, a yellow squash, a red eggplant, a green bottle labeled 'LECHINO', and a small bowl. As he speaks, a smartphone interface overlays the right side of the frame, displaying a 'Log My Weight' screen from a nutrition tracking app. He inputs '165.0 lbs' for April 1, 2023, and taps 'Add Entry'. The app then shows a summary screen with a weight chart indicating a downward trend and highlights 'YOUR TRENDS IMPROVED' with a 2.5% decrease. The man explains, "I'm like amazing, man, this thing is working," referring to his progress. He describes a hypothetical scenario where a "cheat meal" might cause weight to go "back up to 188," expressing "Terrible. Ah, sad." However, he quickly shifts to the positive, detailing how getting "back to the diet" leads to progress, seeing his weight drop to "185," then "184," and finally "183, a whole new low weigh-in." Pointing to his chest and abdomen, he emphasizes his physical progress while stating, "if you look at this graph that I'm showing you, you can see that the graph is trending downwards," explaining that even with a "cheat meal," it "didn't affect your weight." He then recommends a second tracking method, advising viewers to "do tape measurements" every two weeks for "shoulders, chest, mid-section, belly button, thighs, glutes."

event_blocks.summary: The Shirtless Man, initially in a modern kitchen, advises viewers to take weekly progress photos in front, side, and back poses every Sunday, utilizing weight scale, tape measurement, and photos for informed decisions. Transitioning to cardio, he dismisses complex machines, advocating for simple walking and a daily goal of 8,000 steps, increasing by 500 each week, as illustrated by a split-screen transformation graphic showing weight loss from 280lbs to 198lbs and dynamic shots of road running and squat exercises. He later appears by a large window, wearing red athletic shorts and a black watch, explaining that 10,000 steps can burn approximately 500 calories and tap into fat stores, with a YouTube interface overlay prompting viewer engagement. Close-ups show his bare feet on a compact treadmill near a bonsai tree, emphasizing walking as the sole cardio focus. Holding a glass of water, he reiterates walking's superiority, even without a gym. Seen from behind near a glass display cabinet, he continues his monologue under bright sunlight, reinforcing the message of simple, effective fitness and lifestyle.

He speaks directly to the camera in a clear, instructional male voice, gesturing expressively and emphasizing points with hand movements on his chest and abdomen. His monologue focuses on the benefits of walking as cardio, explaining how it helps burn fat, and suggesting using a walking pad at home as an alternative to the gym.

raw_segments: The Shirtless Man, speaking with a clear, instructional, and confident male voice, opens the video shirtless in a modern, well-lit kitchen, using expressive hand gestures as he advises viewers to "take pictures in one location once a week," demonstrating poses for "front, side, back" and reiterating to "take them every Sunday." He explains how to use "your weight scale, your tape measurement, and the photos" to make "a scientific and smart decision based off of three different variables." Transitioning, he announces, "Now, let's talk about cardio. What to do with cardio, follow me." A split-screen transformation graphic then displays a man's body before and after weight loss, showing '280lbs' and '198lbs' with various body measurements, while the man's voice states, "We have the diet part figured out." The Shirtless Man reappears by a large window overlooking a city, continuing his narrative, explaining, "Now the next part is your cardio. Forget the hit, forget all the different technical, cool, like cardio machines," and advises to "Keep it simple. I just want you to walk every day." A 'STEP 5: CARDIO' title card appears, followed by dynamic shots of him running on a winding road and performing a squat in a gym, as he instructs, "I want you to hit 8,000 daily steps." A title card then reads 'PHASE: 1 WEEK 1 8,000 STEPS', while he adds, "And every week increase it by 500." He is then seen walking on a treadmill in a modern gym, illustrating his point, "Week one, when you start, once you've done the diet, walk 8,000 steps." The segment concludes with him back by the window, gesturing near a glass display cabinet, stating, "If you walk more, no problem."

The video opens with the Shirtless Man, wearing a red long-sleeve shirt and red athletic pants, walking on a treadmill in a modern gym setting. His clear, instructional male voice immediately begins a monologue, advising viewers to "just walk" and "increase that by 500 each and every single week," explaining how "10,000 steps will roughly help you burn 500 calories." He then removes his shirt, revealing a muscular, defined torso, and stands by a large window overlooking a cityscape, continuing to speak directly to the camera while gesturing expressively with his hands. He wears a black watch on his left wrist and red athletic shorts. As he speaks about walking tapping into fat stores and burning more fat, a YouTube interface overlay appears at the bottom of the screen, showing 'Like', 'Dislike', 'Subscribe', and 'Subscribed' buttons with a bell icon, indicating a call to action. The scene transitions to a close-up of his bare feet walking on a compact, foldable treadmill placed near a window, with a small bonsai tree beside it, the distinct sound of his footsteps audible as he continues to advocate for walking as the "only cardio you want to focus on." He then reappears, holding a glass of water, continuing to speak and gesture, emphasizing that walking is the "best cardio by far" and doesn't require going to the gym. The camera cuts to a different angle showing him from behind, standing near a glass display cabinet with a white towel draped over a nearby surface. He continues his monologue, emphasizing points by touching his chest and abdomen, all under bright, natural sunlight streaming through the large windows, creating a clean, modern atmosphere. The video ends with him still speaking, maintaining eye contact with the camera, reinforcing his message about simple, effective fitness and lifestyle.

event_blocks.summary: A shirtless man, wearing red athletic shorts and a black watch, stands in a sunlit room with large windows, explaining that fitness shouldn't be overcomplicated, advising incline walking and high-intensity interval training only once or twice a week once body fat reaches 15-20%, a strategy he calls his "client's insurance policy" for losing 20-24 pounds. He then rides a stationary bike outdoors, concluding cardio is sorted. Returning indoors, he transitions to resistance training, with a title card 'STEP 6: RESISTANCE TRAINING' appearing. In a gym setting, he demonstrates cable rows and dumbbell presses, emphasizing training a minimum of three and a maximum of six times a week, suggesting a full-body program on Monday, Wednesday, and Friday if three days are feasible. The video then shifts focus to recovery, with a 'STEP 7: RECOVERY' title card. The man, now in black Adidas shorts in a bedroom, likens sleep to oil for a Ferrari, crucial for optimal testosterone, hormonal balance, and muscle repair, stating that insufficient sleep can hinder weight loss and increase cortisol. He advises a minimum of 7 hours of sleep, with tips including setting a bedtime, avoiding food four hours and liquids two hours before bed, reducing blue light exposure, and avoiding caffeine after midday. He is shown in a dimly lit bedroom using his smartphone and reading a book, reinforcing recovery practices. He concludes by hoping the video was helpful, encouraging likes, comments, subscriptions, and sharing, before a YouTube interface overlay appears and the video ends on a black screen.

Later, he is seen in a black tank top, sitting up in bed under a patterned blanket, using his smartphone, and then wearing glasses while reading a book in a dimly lit bedroom. He concludes his speech with increasing energy, spreading his arms wide, encouraging viewer engagement and saying "Peace."

raw_segments: The video opens with a muscular, shirtless man, wearing red athletic shorts and a black watch, standing in a modern, sunlit room with large windows overlooking a cityscape. He speaks directly to the camera with expressive hand gestures, emphasizing points on his chest and abdomen, explaining how to avoid making fitness "overcomplicated" and advising viewers to start "incline walking" and "high intensity interval training" once they reach 15-20% body fat, but only "once or twice a week," not "every day." He continues, stating that "doing this will help you lose 20 to 24 pounds on its own," calling it his "client's insurance policy." The scene then cuts to a low-angle shot of him riding a black stationary bike outdoors on a covered patio with greenery and stone walls, now wearing blue shorts and a black cap, as he concludes that "Now, we have cardio sorted." The video returns to the indoor setting where he continues speaking, transitioning to "Let's talk about training," which is immediately followed by a title card reading 'STEP 6: RESISTANCE TRAINING' in bold black text on a textured white background. He then appears in a gym setting, performing a cable row exercise while wearing a black tank top and black shorts, then lying on a bench doing dumbbell presses with 20-pound weights, while explaining that "at a minimum, you want to train three times in a week," and "at a max, I want you to train six times a week." He asks, "realistically, how much can you do in a week?" before cutting back to him speaking in the indoor setting, red shorts visible again. He advises that if one can do three days, "the best training split for you is a full body training program," specifying "Full body on Monday, Wednesday, Friday." The sequence ends with a close-up of his hands gripping a cable machine handle during the row exercise, highlighting his muscular arms and focused expression, reinforcing the resistance training theme.

The video opens with a muscular, shirtless man, identified as Mike, in a modern, sunlit room, speaking directly to the camera in a clear male voice. He gestures expressively, touching his chest and abdomen, as he explains training splits, starting with a scenario for training 'four times in a week.' A graphic titled '4 DAY TRAINING SPLIT' appears, illustrating his suggested schedule of 'UPPER BODY' and 'LOWER BODY' days, before Mike reappears, reinforcing the four-day split by holding up four fingers. He then details a five-day split, with the graphic updating to show 'PUSH', 'PULL', and 'LEGS' days, which he elaborates on by listing specific muscle groups like chest, shoulders, triceps for 'PUSH' and back, biceps for 'PULL', and quads, hams, glutes for 'LEGS'. Mike mentions other options, including a full-body split and a six-day 'push, pull, legs' repeat. He transitions to emphasizing the importance of 'good form' and 'intensity,' urging viewers to 'watch me do my form here.' The scene cuts to a gym setting where Mike, now in a black tank top and shorts, demonstrates a Dumbbell Press Exercise, a Squat Exercise, and a Cable Row Exercise, showcasing proper technique. Returning to the window setting, he holds up five fingers while continuing to speak about training with intensity and consistency 'each and every single week' to 'build your muscle.' He performs a Leg Press Exercise in the gym, accompanied by a 'FEET PLACEMENT FOR LEG PRESS' graphic illustrating different foot positions. The video concludes with Mike back by the window, stating, 'it's that simple. Don't complicate it. Good form,' followed by a final shot of him standing shirtless on a balcony.

The segment opens with a muscular, shirtless man, wearing red athletic shorts and a black watch, standing in a bright, modern room with large windows. He speaks directly to the camera, concluding a thought and then introducing the topic of sleep, stating, "And then finally, guys. The way I like describing sleep..." A title card then appears, reading 'STEP 7: RECOVERY' in bold black text on a white background, as the man's voice continues. He reappears in a different bedroom setting, now in black Adidas shorts, sitting on the edge of a bed with white linens, stretching and massaging his neck and shoulders, visually demonstrating recovery. He speaks expressively, using hand gestures, explaining, "Your body is a Ferrari. It is the most well-designed organism on this earth. And sleep is the equivalent to the oil in your Ferrari." He emphasizes that without enough sleep, like a Ferrari without oil, "it's not going to make it down the road. It's going to break down." He elaborates on the benefits, stating that more sleep leads to "optimal levels of testosterone," "good hormonal balance," and "good recovery from the damage you've done to your muscle. Your muscle repairs itself when you're sleeping." He adds that it allows one to "think faster, think better. It fixes everything." The video cuts to a black screen with yellow text, 'LESS SLEEP LEAD TO DECREASING TESTOSTERONE' and 'TESTOSTERONE IS THE MAIN MUSCLE BUILDING HORMONE', accompanied by a molecular structure diagram, while the man's voice shares, "I've had clients who I just focus on their sleep in the beginning... and I got them to seven to eight, and the weight started flying off because there's so many implications that sleep has." He is then shown sleeping peacefully under white covers in the bedroom, reinforcing the message of rest. The scene returns to him speaking in the bedroom, gesturing with his hands and pointing upward, maintaining an engaging and informative tone as he concludes, "So if you're sleeping little and you're trying everything, that's probably the reason why you're not losing weight. It will drop your cortisol levels, it will do so much. I could make..."

The video opens with a muscular, shirtless man, wearing red athletic shorts and a black watch, speaking directly to the camera in a well-lit bedroom. He articulately advises on sleep, emphasizing the importance of 7+ hours, stating, "at bare minimum, sleep 7 hours. If you can sleep more, then sleep more." He continues with general tips like setting a bedtime, stopping food four hours before bed, and liquids two hours prior, and reducing blue light exposure, suggesting to "stop looking at screens or get a blue light blocker." He also warns against caffeine after midday, explaining, "'Cause you don't want to be drinking caffeine at 4 PM and you're wondering why your your heart's going bump bump bump when you when you're in bed and it's just going like crazy." During his speech, he gestures expressively, touching his chest and abdomen. The scene briefly cuts to black before transitioning to a dimly lit bedroom where he is now wearing a black tank top, sitting up in bed under a patterned blanket, using his smartphone under the soft glow of a warm lamp. The video then cuts back to the bright, daytime setting by the window, where he continues speaking with animated hand gestures. Another cut shows him in the same dim bedroom, now wearing glasses and reading a book, with a smartphone resting beside him. The scene returns to the bright window setting, where he speaks with increasing energy, spreading his arms wide in a gesture of openness as he concludes, saying, "I'll leave the video here.
